scenario: You work as a developer for a company. You are using Power Automate to automatically create a lead in Microsoft Dataverse when a new lead gets registered on the company website. While creating the lead in Power Automate, you experience the issue shown in the exhibit (the create action fails because a value being set does not resolve to an existing related record). You need to determine the possible root cause of this issue.
q: Which column should you check to identify the issue's root cause?
why: Check the Lookup column. A lookup stores a reference (GUID) to a row in a related table, so when the flow sets a lookup it must bind to an existing related record (using the correct 'value@odata.bind' / record id). If the provided value does not match an existing related row, the create fails - that is the typical root cause when a Power Automate create-row action errors. Choice/Choices (option sets) and Currency columns store simple values and would not cause this relationship-binding failure.
